description: Resolve FGAP-007: add endpoint-role metadata to ordering relation_types, enforce ordering-edge direction at write time in append-relation — a backwards ordering edge is REJECTED at append, never auto-oriented (the reverted 2026-06-06 attempt's always-swap auto-orientation inverted canonically-authored edges; a08e586) — reconcile the relation_type name with the deriver contract, extend to the registered sibling ordering relations, and migrate any inverted edges.
acceptance_criteria: task_depends_on_task and the currentState deriver agree on parent/child direction: no surface where the name's implied direction is opposite the direction the deriver consumes (name reads prerequisite->dependent matching {parent=prerequisite, child=dependent}, or explicit endpoint-role metadata makes the depender/depended-upon endpoint unambiguous regardless of the verb).

The relation_type registry entry for task_depends_on_task carries explicit endpoint-role metadata identifying which endpoint is the prerequisite and which is the dependent (extending the existing source_kinds/target_kinds metadata).

append-relation validates an ordering edge's parent/child direction against that endpoint-role metadata at write time: a direction-correct ordering edge is accepted; a backwards ordering edge is REJECTED at append with a message naming the expected orientation — never silently stored and never auto-oriented.

The rejection fires only for relation_types carrying the ordering endpoint-role metadata; ordering relation_types without it, and non-ordering relation_types, are unaffected (no new false-positive rejections).

Every existing task_depends_on_task edge in relations.json remains valid after the fix: none orphaned, dropped, or left inverted; any edge whose stored direction was backwards under the corrected contract is migrated to the correct orientation; any already-correct edge is unchanged.
context-validate (relations integrity) passes against the active substrate after the fix and after any edge migration, with no new integrity findings introduced.

The deriver's blocked/ready output for existing tasks is unchanged where edges were already correct, and corrected where an edge was inverted: the fix changes no correctly-derived dependency outcome and only repairs inverted ones.

The same direction enforcement applies to every registered sibling ordering relation_type sharing the inverted-name shape (story_depends_on_story, feature_depends_on_item, feature_gated_by_item, story_gated_by_item, requirement_depends_on_requirement): each either carries the endpoint-role metadata and is enforced, or is demonstrably exempt because its name/derivation are already consistent; none is left with a name-vs-deriver inversion.

Tests assert the write-path direction enforcement: a backwards ordering edge is rejected, a forwards edge is accepted, the metadata-absent gate holds (no enforcement when role metadata is absent), and the deriver continues to treat {parent=prerequisite, child=dependent} correctly.
Build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.
